VAL-INSTALL-002.evidence: Running `mors setup-shell --decline` with isolated HOME shows preview text: 'mors setup-shell will add the following to <home>/.zshrc: export PATH="/usr/local/bin:$PATH" # mors' followed by 'This adds the mors binary directory to your PATH'. The command previews the edit BEFORE requiring confirmation. JSON output with --decline shows status:declined with shell, rcFile, binDir metadata. Exit code 0.

VAL-INSTALL-003.evidence: Created .zshrc with known content, computed MD5 before. Ran `mors setup-shell --decline`. After decline: .zshrc MD5 identical to before. JSON output confirms status:declined, applied:false. Terminal output says 'No changes applied.' Exit code 0.

VAL-INSTALL-004.evidence: First confirm run: JSON status:applied, applied:true. .zshrc updated with single line 'export PATH="<bindir>:$PATH" # mors'. Second confirm run: JSON status:already_configured with message 'PATH is already configured for mors. No changes needed.' .zshrc content identical after second run. grep -c '# mors' shows exactly 1 line — idempotent. Exit code 0 for both runs.
